capability_objective: For a read-only operations check, Search separately for “Accessibility Council Check-in” in Denver and “Grant Budget Review” in Chicago, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete meeting records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Perform a read-only meeting operations check in the supplied sandbox and create `meeting-check.md`. Read `README.md` first, then use the genuine local `./meeting-registry` executable through Pi's Bash tool as the sole interface to meeting records and notifications. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or generated runtime data, or its audit evidence. Do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under `.protected`; running the verification command named below is the only permitted access to that directory. Do not manufacture execution evidence or create a helper script, function, loop, wrapper, or scratch file.

Locate the exact meeting named `Accessibility Council Check-in` in `Denver` and the exact meeting named `Grant Budget Review` in `Chicago`. Every reported value must be grounded by first searching the registry and then retrieving the complete current record by the unique stable ID returned by that meeting's search. The searches are independent: start them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct registry operation in each call. Do not combine them in one shell call and do not launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Continue only if each search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing, ambiguous, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved lookup without guessing, and do not create the report.

Only after both searches have completed successfully, retrieve the two complete records using only their respective returned IDs. Start the retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that accesses meeting data, again with one direct registry operation in each call. Do not begin either retrieval until both searches have finished. If either retrieval fails, does not correspond to its search result, or lacks a status or date, stop and do not create the report. Base the comparison and every deliverable value only on the two complete records returned by retrieval, never on search summaries, this request, or prior knowledge.

This check is strictly read-only. Invoke no registry operation except the two required searches and the two eligible retrievals. Do not create, update, cancel, or notify any meeting. Do not alter requested, distractor, archived, or related records; notifications; backing or runtime data; audit evidence; or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events. Apart from `meeting-check.md` and runtime files created automatically by the required registry reads, do not create, modify, or delete any file or directory.

When both complete records have been retrieved successfully, create `meeting-check.md` as the only file you write directly. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank plain-text sentences with no heading, bullets, or extra prose. Put Accessibility Council Check-in first and Grant Budget Review second. Format the first two lines exactly as `<name> in <city> (<stable_id>) has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by each complete record. Format the third line exactly as `Comparison: statuses <match-or-differ> (<Accessibility-status> versus <Grant-status>), and dates <match-or-differ> (<Accessibility-date> versus <Grant-date>).`, replacing each `match-or-differ` with `match` when the corresponding returned values are equal and `differ` otherwise. Include no other fields, inference, recommendation, or action. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the report. Return exactly the report content as the final response.
